refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability ≥ 0.999 (99.9%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link is operational to total evaluation time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-010 addresses a critical operational requirement: remote equipment control depends on reliable communication. The refined FR quantifies the acceptance criterion (≥0.999 availability), specifies the measurement scope (bidirectional link), and defines the evaluation window. This is essential for safety-critical teleoperation where link loss can lead to equipment immobilization or unsafe states.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous monitoring of link state (up/down) during the evaluation period; calculate availability as (total_time_link_up) / (total_evaluation_time). Log link state transitions with timestamps. Compare measured availability against the 0.999 threshold at evaluation conclusion.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within [X] milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe shutdown or hold mode upon link loss.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall support redundant or failover communication paths to achieve target availability.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log and report link availability metrics for post-evaluation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement dual independent communication channels (primary + backup) with automatic failover; monitor link health continuously; log availability metrics.
DP-B: Implement single primary communication channel with redundancy at the network infrastructure layer (carrier-provided SLA); rely on external network availability guarantees.
DP-C: Implement adaptive link management with dynamic path selection, multi-carrier aggregation, and local edge caching to mask transient outages.
DP-D: Implement software-defined networking (SDN) overlay with in-band link monitoring, automatic rerouting, and per-packet redundancy encoding.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Implement dual independent communication channels (primary + backup) with automatic failover; monitor link health continuously; log availability metrics.

potential_sources: DP-A couples teleoperation link availability to the Vehicle's communication hardware (dual modems/antennas) and to the Teleoperation module's failover logic.
DP-A couples to Safety Services for safe-state enforcement upon link loss.
DP-A couples to the Network Link interface for both primary and backup paths.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is acceptable and necessary: dual channels require hardware support (Vehicle), failover logic resides in Teleoperation, and safety response is coordinated with Safety Services. No circular or hidden dependencies detected. DP-A is decoupled from specific carrier or network topology, allowing flexibility in implementation.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Two independent communication channels (e.g., cellular + satellite, or dual cellular carriers) are available at the worksite.
ASSUMPTION: Automatic failover logic can be implemented with latency < [X] ms to avoid safety violations.
ASSUMPTION: Link health monitoring (heartbeat, signal strength) is reliable and does not itself introduce false positives.
RISK: If both channels fail simultaneously, availability target cannot be met; mitigation requires geographic/temporal diversity in channel selection.
RISK: Failover latency may exceed operator reaction time; mitigation requires safe-state design (e.g., hold mode) that does not depend on operator intervention.
RISK: Dual-channel hardware cost and power consumption may exceed project budget; trade-off analysis required.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify: (1) channel independence criteria (e.g., different carriers, different frequency bands, geographic separation); (2) failover latency budget (e.g., < 100 ms); (3) safe-state behavior upon link loss (e.g., hold current position, engage brakes); (4) link health monitoring algorithm (e.g., heartbeat interval, timeout threshold).

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The Teleoperation module owns the dual-channel communication architecture, failover logic, link health monitoring, and availability metrics logging. It interfaces with the Vehicle for command transmission and telemetry reception, with Safety Services for safe-state coordination, and with the Network Link for both primary and backup communication paths.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands (movement, tool control, etc.) from the tele-operator; routed through primary or backup channel depending on link state.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state (position, sensor readings, equipment status) to the tele-operator; sourced from Vehicle module; routed through primary or backup channel.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and link-loss alerts from Safety Services; transmits safe-state confirmation and link availability metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Primary and backup communication paths; Teleoperation monitors link health, initiates failover, and logs link state transitions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of dual-channel failover logic, link health monitoring, and availability metrics. High containment: all failover decision logic, channel selection, and metrics logging reside here. Minimal diffusion: only interfaces to Vehicle (for command/telemetry routing) and Safety Services (for safe-state coordination) are external.

Vehicle owns the dual communication hardware (modems, antennas) but does not own failover logic or link monitoring. If DP-A were assigned to Vehicle, failover logic would be split between Vehicle and Teleoperation, creating diffusion and coupling. Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safe-state enforcement but not link availability management. Assigning DP-A to Safety Services would create tight coupling and diffusion of concerns. Safety Services should react to link-loss alerts from Teleoperation, not manage the link itself.

NGHE Solution is the system-level container but is too coarse-grained to own DP-A. Assigning here would defer architectural decisions and create diffusion across multiple sub-modules. Not recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the optimal owner of DP-A because: (1) it logically owns the command-and-telemetry link abstraction; (2) failover logic is a core teleoperation concern, not a vehicle or safety concern; (3) link health monitoring and metrics logging are teleoperation responsibilities; (4) external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all natural to Teleoperation's scope.
containment_risks: RISK: If Vehicle hardware (dual modems) is not available or is not independently controllable, Teleoperation cannot implement failover. Mitigation: Vehicle module must expose independent control of primary and backup channels via a new interface (e.g., 'ChannelControl').

RISK: If Safety Services does not respond quickly to link-loss alerts, safe-state enforcement may be delayed. Mitigation: define SafetyAlert interface with strict latency requirements (e.g., safe-state engaged within 100 ms of link-loss notification).

RISK: Teleoperation module may become too large if it also owns other communication concerns (e.g., encryption, compression). Mitigation: decompose Teleoperation into sub-modules (e.g., LinkManager, FailoverController, MetricsLogger) at the next level.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Vehicle module exposes independent control of primary and backup communication channels. Define SafetyAlert interface with explicit latency and response requirements. Consider decomposing Teleoperation into sub-modules at the next level to manage complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (dual-channel failover with continuous monitoring) directly addresses FR-NGHE-010 (achieve ≥0.999 link availability). The DP is specific, measurable, and implementable. No logical gaps or misalignments detected.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk: Teleoperation owns failover logic and monitoring; Vehicle owns hardware; Safety Services owns safe-state enforcement. Clear separation of concerns.
Interface risk: If Vehicle does not expose independent channel control, Teleoperation cannot implement failover. Mitigation: confirm Vehicle interface design before proceeding.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation containment score is 0.95, indicating strong alignment. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all well-defined and necessary. No artificial module boundaries or missing interfaces detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: link availability can be measured directly via continuous monitoring and logging. Acceptance criterion (≥0.999) is quantitative and testable. Evaluation period and measurement methodology must be defined in the test plan.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Teleoperation owns DP-A implementation; Vehicle provides dual-channel hardware; Safety Services provides safe-state enforcement. Responsibilities are distinct and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-A to specify failover latency budget, safe-state behavior, and link health monitoring algorithm.
Confirm Vehicle module interface design for independent channel control.
Define SafetyAlert interface with explicit latency and response requirements.
Plan decomposition of Teleoperation module at the next level to manage complexity.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-010 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (link loss detection, safe shutdown, redundant paths, metrics logging). DP-A is sound and well-aligned with Teleoperation module. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to define specific latency budgets, safe-state behaviors, and monitoring algorithms. Proceeding to the next level will refine these concerns and validate the architecture.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within [X] milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe shutdown or hold mode upon link loss.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall support redundant or failover communication paths to achieve target availability.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log and report link availability metrics for post-evaluation analysis.

assumptions: Two independent communication channels are available at the worksite.
Automatic failover logic can be implemented with latency < [X] ms.
Link health monitoring is reliable and does not introduce false positives.
Vehicle module exposes independent control of primary and backup channels.
Safety Services responds to link-loss alerts within [X] ms.
open_risks: Simultaneous failure of both channels; mitigation requires geographic/temporal diversity.
Failover latency may exceed operator reaction time; mitigation requires safe-state design.
Dual-channel hardware cost and power consumption may exceed budget.
Vehicle interface design may not support independent channel control.
SafetyAlert interface latency may be insufficient for safe-state enforcement.
evidence_needed: Vehicle module interface specification for dual-channel control.
SafetyAlert interface specification with latency requirements.
Link health monitoring algorithm design and validation.
Cost and power consumption estimates for dual-channel hardware.
Test plan for link availability measurement and evaluation.
